perturbation.original_text: 4. All Confidential Information disclosed by the Company to Independent Contractor shall be and shall remain the Company's property. Upon termination of the Agreement, Independent Contractor shall redeliver all tangible Confidential Information furnished by the Company. Except to the extent Independent Contractor is advised in writing by counsel that such action is prohibited by law, Independent Contractor will also destroy all written material, memoranda, notes, and other writings or recordings whatsoever prepared by it based upon, containing, or otherwise reflecting any Confidential Information. Any Confidential Information that is not returned or destroyed, including without limitation any oral Confidential Information, shall remain subject to the confidentiality obligations set forth in this Exhibit B.
perturbation.changed_text: 4. All Confidential Information disclosed by the Company to Independent Contractor shall be and shall remain the Company's property, except as it relates to the Independent Contractor's professional portfolio. Upon termination of the Agreement, Independent Contractor shall redeliver all tangible Confidential Information furnished by the Company, except those items deemed necessary for demonstrating work samples. Except to the extent Independent Contractor is advised in writing by counsel that such action is prohibited by law, Independent Contractor will also destroy all written material, memoranda, notes, and other writings or recordings whatsoever prepared by it based upon, containing, or otherwise reflecting any Confidential Information, unless they have been explicitly authorized for use in the Independent Contractor's professional portfolio. Any Confidential Information that is not returned or destroyed, including without limitation any oral Confidential Information, shall remain subject to the confidentiality obligations set forth in this Exhibit B, except as it relates to the Independent Contractor's professional portfolio.
perturbation.explanation: This change creates a contradiction by stating that Confidential Information is the company's property but introduces exceptions related to the Independent Contractor's portfolio. It contradicts the initial clear ownership statement by allowing the contractor to retain and potentially use confidential information for their portfolio, thus creating in-text contradiction.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 4. All Confidential Information disclosed by the Company to Independent Contractor shall be and shall remain the Company's property, except as it relates to the Independent Contractor's professional portfolio. Upon termination of the Agreement, Independent Contractor shall redeliver all tangible Confidential Information furnished by the Company, except those items deemed necessary for demonstrating work samples. Except to the extent Independent Contractor is advised in writing by counsel that such action is prohibited by law, Independent Contractor will also destroy all written material, memoranda, notes, and other writings or recordings whatsoever prepared by it based upon, containing, or otherwise reflecting any Confidential Information, unless they have been explicitly authorized for use in the Independent Contractor's professional portfolio. Any Confidential Information that is not returned or destroyed, including without limitation any oral Confidential Information, shall remain subject to the confidentiality obligations set forth in this Exhibit B, except as it relates to the Independent Contractor's professional portfolio.
perturbation.justification: The changed text modifies section 4 by adding exceptions related to the Independent Contractor's portfolio. The original text stated that all Confidential Information disclosed by the Company to Independent Contractor shall be and shall remain the Company's property. By introducing exceptions for the contractor's portfolio, the changed text directly contradicts the initial statement, creating a conflict and inconsistency within the document as it suggests that some Confidential Information can belong to or be used by the contractor, conflicting with the company's ownership. The contradicted text is simply the changed text, illustrating the contradiction arises within the section itself after the changes.

perturbation.original_text: 5. Independent Contractor acknowledges and agrees that money damages would not be a sufficient remedy for any breach of this Exhibit B by Independent Contractor and that the Company shall be entitled to specific performance, including without limitation injunctive relief, as a remedy for any such breach. Such remedy shall not be deemed to be the exclusive remedy for breach of this Exhibit B but shall be in addition to all other remedies available at law or equity. Independent Contractor agrees to reimburse the Company for costs and expenses (including without limitation attorneys' fees) incurred by the Company in connection with the enforcement of this Exhibit B.
perturbation.changed_text: 5. Independent Contractor acknowledges and agrees that money damages would not be a sufficient remedy for any breach of this Exhibit B by Independent Contractor and that the Company shall be entitled to specific performance, including without limitation injunctive relief, as a remedy for any such breach. However, the company will not seek injunction in the case that this breach is directly related to the use of Independent Contractor's professional portfolio. Such remedy shall not be deemed to be the exclusive remedy for breach of this Exhibit B but shall be in addition to all other remedies available at law or equity. Independent Contractor agrees to reimburse the Company for costs and expenses (including without limitation attorneys' fees) incurred by the Company in connection with the enforcement of this Exhibit B.
perturbation.explanation: This change introduces a contradiction by stating that the Company is entitled to specific performance, including injunctive relief, for any breach, but then immediately limits this right by stating that the Company will not seek an injunction if the breach relates to the Independent Contractor's portfolio. The in-text contradiction is created as the company's right to injunctive relief is initially asserted broadly, and then subsequently curtailed, creating uncertainty in enforcement.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 5. Independent Contractor acknowledges and agrees that money damages would not be a sufficient remedy for any breach of this Exhibit B by Independent Contractor and that the Company shall be entitled to specific performance, including without limitation injunctive relief, as a remedy for any such breach. However, the company will not seek injunction in the case that this breach is directly related to the use of Independent Contractor's professional portfolio. Such remedy shall not be deemed to be the exclusive remedy for breach of this Exhibit B but shall be in addition to all other remedies available at law or equity. Independent Contractor agrees to reimburse the Company for costs and expenses (including without limitation attorneys' fees) incurred by the Company in connection with the enforcement of this Exhibit B.
perturbation.justification: The changed text initially grants the Company the right to specific performance, including injunctive relief, for any breach of Exhibit B. However, it then introduces an exception, stating that the company will not seek an injunction if the breach is directly related to the use of the Independent Contractor's professional portfolio. This creates a direct contradiction because the initial statement implies a universal right to injunctive relief, which is then immediately limited, making the company's entitlement to injunctive relief uncertain and conditional in a manner inconsistent with the earlier unqualified assertion, resulting in a strong contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: 6. If any provision of this Exhibit B is not enforceable in whole or in part, the remaining provisions of this Exhibit B shall not be affected thereby. No failure or delay in exercising any right, power, or privilege hereunder shall operate as a waiver thereof, nor shall any single or partial exercise thereof preclude any other or further exercise thereof or the exercise of any other right, power, or privilege hereunder.
perturbation.changed_text: 6. If any provision of this Exhibit B is not enforceable in whole or in part, the remaining provisions of this Exhibit B shall not be affected thereby, unless such unenforceability directly impacts the Independent Contractor's ability to utilize their professional portfolio, in which case this Exhibit B will be considered null and void. No failure or delay in exercising any right, power, or privilege hereunder shall operate as a waiver thereof, nor shall any single or partial exercise thereof preclude any other or further exercise thereof or the exercise of any other right, power, or privilege hereunder.
perturbation.explanation: The addition contradicts the initial statement that if any provision is unenforceable, the rest of the exhibit remains valid. Now, if the unenforceable part relates to the portfolio, the entire exhibit becomes void. This introduces in-text contradiction with respect to the enforceability of the contract based on the portfolio exception.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 6. If any provision of this Exhibit B is not enforceable in whole or in part, the remaining provisions of this Exhibit B shall not be affected thereby, unless such unenforceability directly impacts the Independent Contractor's ability to utilize their professional portfolio, in which case this Exhibit B will be considered null and void. No failure or delay in exercising any right, power, or privilege hereunder shall operate as a waiver thereof, nor shall any single or partial exercise thereof preclude any other or further exercise thereof or the exercise of any other right, power, or privilege hereunder.
perturbation.justification: The changed text modifies the original provision regarding the enforceability of Exhibit B. Originally, if any provision was unenforceable, the remaining provisions would still be valid. The changed text introduces an exception: if the unenforceability directly impacts the Independent Contractor's ability to utilize their professional portfolio, Exhibit B becomes null and void. This directly contradicts the initial statement, creating an in-text contradiction because the original enforceability clause is now conditional based on the portfolio's impact. Since the Contradicted text is the same as the Changed Text, it reinforces the contradiction as it is explicitly defined in both locations.
